title: When Things Go Wrong
knowledge_note: Every failure is a lesson. The Quran says 'with hardship comes ease.' Handle errors gracefully and your programs will be stronger.
explanation: A 'try' block runs code that might fail. If something goes wrong, the 'when wrong' block catches the error and lets you recover gracefully.
starter_code:
try:
    shout "test error"
when wrong:
    say "Something went wrong, but I handled it!"
worked_example:
try:
    shout "test error"
when wrong:
    say "Something went wrong, but I handled it!"
challenge: Try dividing by zero inside a try block.
hint: Put say 10 / 0 inside the try block.
expected_output: Something went wrong, but I handled it!
